Using DBeaver IDE with SQL

Using DBeaver with DuckDB

This guide shows how to use the database IDE DBeaver with DuckDB.

DuckDB tutorial on DBeaver:

Motivation

Most jobs that require SQL will not have you using RStudio to run SQL queries. Instead, you will typically work with a database IDE.

Let’s try another IDE designed specifically for interacting with databases.

Setup

The instructions here are specifically for DuckDB, but DBeaver works with many other database systems such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite.

1. Install DBeaver

Download and install DBeaver Community Edition:

2. Create a DuckDB Connection

When you open DBeaver, you should see a window asking you to choose a database type.

Select DuckDB.

If you do not see this window:

  1. Open DBeaver.
  2. From the top menu, select Database.
  3. Click New Database Connection.
  4. A window will open where you can choose the database type.
  5. Select DuckDB.

3. Connect to the Database File

On the next screen:

  1. Make sure Host is selected.
  2. Navigate to your flights.duckdb database file.
  3. Click Finish.

In the Path field you need to provide the absolute path on your computer where flights.duckdb has been saved.

Example:

/Users/yourname/data/flights.duckdb

4. Install the DuckDB Driver (First Time Only)

Double-click the database connection flights.duckdb.

If you have not used DuckDB before, DBeaver will ask you to install a driver in order to connect.

Just click Download / Install.

5. Create a SQL File

Once connected, you should see the database in the left panel.

To run SQL queries:

  1. Right-click on the connection.
  2. Select SQL Editor → New SQL Script.

6. Run SQL Queries

Type some SQL and run it by:

  • Clicking the Execute button, or

  • Pressing the keyboard shortcut:

  • Control + Enter (Windows/Linux)

  • Command + Enter (Mac)

7. Export Data

To save query results:

  1. Right-click the results table.
  2. Select Export Data.
  3. Choose CSV or another format.

8. Disconnect

  • How to Disconnect and Reconnect a Database in DBeaver

  1. On the top menu, click Database.

  2. From the drop-down menu, select Disconnect.

  3. You should see a message on the bottom right corner:

    Not connected to database

  4. To reconnect, go to Database → Connect and select your connection.

This is the easiest way to manage connections without closing DBeaver or losing work.

Notes

DBeaver has many additional features such as:

  • browsing tables
  • viewing schemas
  • query history
  • data editing

We will not cover them here. Most of the interface is intuitive, so spend some time exploring and experimenting with the tool.
